title: TTPO deprovision: staggered SSO retirement, MDM re-enroll, and courier-hold for cross-border departure
preview:
Issue summary:
HR has requested a two-week temporary passover (TTPO) deprovision for an employee relocating overseas mid-contract. Request is to stagger account deactivation so service accounts and SSO remain available for knowledge-transfer tasks until the courier confirms laptop collection. HR flagged export/compl...
text:
Issue summary:
HR has requested a two-week temporary passover (TTPO) deprovision for an employee relocating overseas mid-contract. Request is to stagger account deactivation so service accounts and SSO remain available for knowledge-transfer tasks until the courier confirms laptop collection. HR flagged export/compliance concerns for the laptop transit and asked Asset Management to place a hold with the courier.

Impact:
- Potential risk of accidental early SSO/account revocation interrupting handover work (engineering + documentation updates).
- Hardware custody must remain in chain-of-custody due to encryption keys tied to device KMS tags.
- Cross-border courier requires explicit KYC and export paperwork before release.

Environment:
- Internal accounts: SSO (Okta), GSuite, GitHub org, AWS console role.
- Device managed by MDM (Jamf).
- Employee tier: full-time engineering (access t...

title: Copy Query permalink returns 404 when retention backfill job rotates tracehook tokens
preview: Issue summary: Customer reports that the 'Copy Query as Permalink' action in the Console returns a 404 when the team runs a retention backfill that triggers tracehook token rotation. Impact: external stakeholders using saved permalinks from scheduled reports get 404s and cannot access traces referenced in alerts. En...
text: Issue summary: Customer reports that the 'Copy Query as Permalink' action in the Console returns a 404 when the team runs a retention backfill that triggers tracehook token rotation. Impact: external stakeholders using saved permalinks from scheduled reports get 404s and cannot access traces referenced in alerts. Environment: prod us-east during a retention backfill window. Affects scheduled report links shared externally.

title: Speculative tool-schema hydration causing delayed streamed chunk delivery and mid-stream timeouts
preview:
Issue summary: Customer reports sharp increase in end-to-end latency for streamed responses when prompts include tool calls. The stream starts but streamed chunks are delayed ~2-8s mid-response, occasionally leading to client-side timeouts.

Impact: Affects multiple production customers (enterprise tier). Intermitte...
text:
Issue summary: Customer reports sharp increase in end-to-end latency for streamed responses when prompts include tool calls. The stream starts but streamed chunks are delayed ~2-8s mid-response, occasionally leading to client-side timeouts.

Impact: Affects multiple production customers (enterprise tier). Intermittent but reproducible with tool call enabled; increases tail latency and causes streaming sessions to abort after client-side timeouts.

Environment: prod cluster in us-east, streaming route /v1/chat/stream with toolcall preflight enabled and schema-hydration flag set to speculative=true. Observed for rw-open-7b-v1 and rw-llama-13b-v2.

Observed behavior: Initial tokens stream normally for ~200-400ms, then a stall of 2-8s while server emits heartbeats but no content chunks. Traces show a synchronous preflight/schema hydration step invoked mid-stream for tool metadata.

Expect...

title: Intermittent generation requests with very large max_tokens time out near token-window boundary (KV-cache refill stall suspected)
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer reports intermittent 504 / request timeouts for single generation requests when using very large max_tokens (typically >= 120k token window). Failures occur late in the generation (after ~70-95% of requested tokens produced). Suspected stall during KV-cache refill / eviction cycle in serving ...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports intermittent 504 / request timeouts for single generation requests when using very large max_tokens (typically >= 120k token window). Failures occur late in the generation (after ~70-95% of requested tokens produced). Suspected stall during KV-cache refill / eviction cycle in serving runtime when prefill and decode phases overlap under heavy batching.

Impact:
- Affects multiple request attempts from NimbusAI production workloads on Dedicated pools in us-east; blocking their long-transcript generation features.
- Symptoms: spike in 5xx (504 gateway timeouts) and client-observed disconnects; partial outputs received before termination.

Environment:
- Dedicated customer pool (dedicated), prod:us-east; models: gptx-16b-fp16 primarily.
- Requests routed through API gateway with default 60s client timeout in their code, but server-side timeouts observed bey...

title: Intermittent 502/503 errors during large embedding uploads due to chunked body aborts at API gateway
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer LexiconSoft reports elevated 502/503 errors and failed embedding upload jobs when sending large batched embedding payloads (~50–200MB) using our REST API over HTTP/2 with chunked transfer encoding. Errors began 2026-03-12 09:10 UTC after a gradual traffic increase.

Impact:
- Several customer...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer LexiconSoft reports elevated 502/503 errors and failed embedding upload jobs when sending large batched embedding payloads (~50–200MB) using our REST API over HTTP/2 with chunked transfer encoding. Errors began 2026-03-12 09:10 UTC after a gradual traffic increase.

Impact:
- Several customer embedding jobs failed (25% failure rate on a 40-job run).
- Increased API error-rate across their tenant (~P50->P95 error increase).
- Affects production P95 latency and caused retry storms from client side.

Environment:
- prod cluster in us-east-1; customer on enterprise plan with Dedicated routing to high-priority pool.
- Requests use HTTP/2 with chunked transfer-encoding and per-request streaming.
- Model: text-embedding-3-large pinned for this customer.

Observed behavior and tentative root cause hypothesis:
- API gateway logs show upstream proxy closing the request s...

title: Main-thread GC starvation in session-sidecar causing cascading 503s and api-gateway retries
preview:
Issue summary:
Customers observed a sudden rise in 5xx responses (primarily 503 / upstream unavailable) from the hosted API in us-west during high-concurrency streaming workloads. Error wave started 2026-03-13 02:12 UTC and lasted ~45 minutes with intermittent recovery windows.

Impact:
- LexiDocs (dedicated tenant)...
text:
Issue summary:
Customers observed a sudden rise in 5xx responses (primarily 503 / upstream unavailable) from the hosted API in us-west during high-concurrency streaming workloads. Error wave started 2026-03-13 02:12 UTC and lasted ~45 minutes with intermittent recovery windows.

Impact:
- LexiDocs (dedicated tenant) reported ~35% of streaming sessions failing with 503s and mid-request disconnects.
- Platform-wide p99 tail latency for text-generation increased from 110ms -> 1.8s for affected hosts.
- API gateway showed elevated retry rates causing localized threadpool exhaustion.

Environment:
prod/us-west, dedicated pool for LexiDocs. Session-sidecars (Python 3.11) present on each node to manage streaming sessions and multipart uploads. Serving runtime (Rust) remained mostly healthy on GPU hosts.

Timeline / Observed signals:
- 02:10 UTC: LexiDocs bulk streaming load begins (concurren...

title: Edge probe split causes half-formed streamed messages after reconnect
preview: Issue summary: Customer reports that after an intermittent network disconnect, SSE streams resume but the first resumed payloads are half-formed JSON fragments and the client parser fails. Impact: Several NexaHealth production workflows ingesting streamed clinical summaries are producing invalid partial objects; cus...
text: Issue summary: Customer reports that after an intermittent network disconnect, SSE streams resume but the first resumed payloads are half-formed JSON fragments and the client parser fails. Impact: Several NexaHealth production workflows ingesting streamed clinical summaries are producing invalid partial objects; customer reports ~15% of resumed sessions encounter parse errors and drops. Environment: prod, us-east, edge-proxy path through third-party CDN probe that occasionally inserts synthetic TCP probe packets. Observed behavior: On reconnect the server sends a resumed chunk that starts mid-UTF8 sequence and omits the JSON array opener; client logs "JSON parse error: unexpected token". Expected behavior: Stream resume should deliver complete, well-delimited framing and finalization events so clients can stitch without parse errors. Business impact: High — downstream ingestion pipeli...

title: Pinned cohort unexpectedly coalesced onto legacy variant during weighted canary cutover, causing mixed outputs and cost spike
preview: Issue summary: During a planned weighted canary cutover for redwood-v2-medium -> redwood-v2-quantized at ~2026-03-13T18:22Z, approximately 7% of Finato Bank's tenant cohort (cohort id c-88ff) that should have been routed to the canary variant was instead routed to the legacy variant pinned from a previous rollout. I...
text:
Issue summary: During a planned weighted canary cutover for redwood-v2-medium -> redwood-v2-quantized at ~2026-03-13T18:22Z, approximately 7% of Finato Bank's tenant cohort (cohort id c-88ff) that should have been routed to the canary variant was instead routed to the legacy variant pinned from a previous rollout. Impact: mixed-model responses, higher than expected token costs, and a small number of failing structured outputs for validation prompts. Severity: customer-visible degradation (inconsistent outputs) and billing spike.

Observed behaviour: traffic that matched the pin cohort showed two routing fingerprints in edge logs; a subset of requests carried a routing cookie keyed to legacy-variant-id=rv2-2025-11 while orchestration snapshots indicated the cohort should map to rv2-q1. The routing mesh appears to have merged cohort metadata during a snapshot reconcile operation which c...

title: Chunked request + oversized tracing headers cause API Gateway proxy buffering and idle timeouts leading to 504s
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer reports intermittent 504 gateway timeouts and increased tail latency for long-running chat completions. Issue appears correlated with clients sending chunked transfer requests and very large tracing/correlation headers (X-Trace-Baggage). Impact:
- ~12% of requests from NimbleDocs in us-west-2...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports intermittent 504 gateway timeouts and increased tail latency for long-running chat completions. Issue appears correlated with clients sending chunked transfer requests and very large tracing/correlation headers (X-Trace-Baggage). Impact:
- ~12% of requests from NimbleDocs in us-west-2 are failing with 504 or returning after >30s delay.
- Affects production inference for redwood/gpt-7b-v2-quant used in assistant completion flows.
Environment:
- Prod, us-west-2, Hosted API (edge -> API Gateway -> NLB -> serving pool)
- API Gateway: AWS ALB in front of Nginx proxy instances (managed by infra team)
- Customer client uses chunked transfer with streaming and attaches large tracing baggage headers (~9–12 KB)
Observed behavior:
- Requests with headers >8KB frequently trigger proxy buffering in Nginx and cause the ALB to hit idle timeout waiting for backend byte...

title: Corporate firewall idle-probe policy terminates long-lived gRPC/control-plane sessions causing streaming interruptions in private deployment

Impact:
- Several production chat sessions dropped every ~3 minutes for users in us-east-1....
text:
Issue summary:
Customer (FinLedger) reports intermittent user-facing streaming breaks for long-lived chat sessions in their private deployment. Breaks correlate with gRPC errors and immediate reconnect attempts from the SDK.

Impact:
- Several production chat sessions dropped every ~3 minutes for users in us-east-1.
- Affects real-time streaming responses and some control-plane heartbeats; customers see partial responses followed by reconnect.
- Customer SLA: private deployment with 24/7 support (enterprise).

Environment:
- Private deployment behind customer-managed Palo Alto NGFW (on-prem firewall + transit GW).
- Redwood control plane + model-serving endpoints over peered subnets (10.10.0.0/16).
- Observed in prod | us-east-1 only.

Steps to reproduce (observed):
1) Start long-running streaming chat session via Redwood SDK to vicuna-13b-stream pinned model.

title: Half-closed stream: trailing clause dropped when client send window shrinks during auth rotation
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer Edify.ai reports that when their browser-based client (web SDK) encounters a rapid shrink of the TCP send window (simulated on flaky mobile networks) concurrent with an opaque auth cookie rotation, the final clause of the generated stream is not returned. The server emits a half-close (FIN) p...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer Edify.ai reports that when their browser-based client (web SDK) encounters a rapid shrink of the TCP send window (simulated on flaky mobile networks) concurrent with an opaque auth cookie rotation, the final clause of the generated stream is not returned. The server emits a half-close (FIN) path but the client-side backpressure and connection rebind cause the terminal chunk (closing punctuation + metadata) to be lost.

Impact:
- Intermittent loss of last sentence or trailing clause in streamed responses for a subset of long outputs (~>1k tokens).
- Affects a small percentage of dedicated-customer sessions in us-east under bursty mobile-proxied traffic.
- Customer-visible: generated text ends mid-sentence and no error/partial completion metadata is delivered.

Environment and context:
- Customer using dedicated capacity with reserved GPUs in us-east-1.
- SDK: re...

title: TS SDK: AbortController cancel + immediate retry loses tool-callback confirmations when using delegated token rotation
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer using the TypeScript SDK observes missing tool/callback confirmations after cancelling a streaming generation with AbortController and then immediately retrying. Behaviour appears correlated with delegated API key rotation in their auth stack: the retried request uses a refreshed delegated to...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer using the TypeScript SDK observes missing tool/callback confirmations after cancelling a streaming generation with AbortController and then immediately retrying. Behaviour appears correlated with delegated API key rotation in their auth stack: the retried request uses a refreshed delegated token and the previously-sent confirmation callback for the first attempt is never reconciled, leading to partial downstream side-effects (duplicate back-office jobs or missing confirmation receipts).

Impact:
- Intermittent data-loss/duplicate-side-effect symptoms in customer workflow (invoice submissions are sometimes missing confirmations, occasionally duplicated).
- Affects a subset of streams where client cancels then retries within ~2s window; estimated ~0.8% of request flow for this customer but hits critical payment paths.

Environment:
- Customer tier: Dedicated pool...

title: Multiplexed streaming 502s during mid-morning tenant burst linked to ephemeral-worker shed
preview: Issue summary: Between 09:20 and 10:05 PDT on 2026-03-13 several customers observed intermittent 502 responses on multiplexed streaming endpoints. The errors coincided with a mid-morning tenant burst from a few heavy tenants on Dedicated pools. Impact: streaming sessions saw partial responses followed by 502s; recon...
text:
Issue summary: Between 09:20 and 10:05 PDT on 2026-03-13 several customers observed intermittent 502 responses on multiplexed streaming endpoints. The errors coincided with a mid-morning tenant burst from a few heavy tenants on Dedicated pools. Impact: streaming sessions saw partial responses followed by 502s; reconnect loops caused visible degradation for ScribeAI (dedicated tenant) and a small set of other tenants. Not a global outage but high error-rate window for affected tenants.

Observed behavior: API gateway returned 502s with upstream read timeout/connection reset. Upstream runtime pods reported short-lived OOM restarts and voluntary eviction of ephemeral worker threads. Autoscaler logs show a rapid shed of ephemeral workers while persistent workers remained. Traffic pattern: long-lived multiplexed HTTP/2 streams with periodic large token bursts (embedding + generation mix).

title: traffic-diversion-to-quantized-fallback-enters-warmup-rack-causing-burst-503s
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer NovaSearch reported a sudden spike in 5xx (503) responses and p99 inference latency starting 2026-03-11 21:10 UTC. Traffic was diverted to a quantized GPU fallback variant that appears to be in a warmup/initializing state on a single rack, causing serial cold-starts and temporary capacity exh...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer NovaSearch reported a sudden spike in 5xx (503) responses and p99 inference latency starting 2026-03-11 21:10 UTC. Traffic was diverted to a quantized GPU fallback variant that appears to be in a warmup/initializing state on a single rack, causing serial cold-starts and temporary capacity exhaustion.

Impact:
- Intermittent 503s and p99 latency > 12s for search reranking calls between 21:10–21:48 UTC.
- Around 18% of requests for particular routes returned 503 during the peak window.
- Customer-facing application experienced degraded search relevance and increased error rates across user sessions.

Environment:
- Redwood hosted dedicated pool (NovaSearch reserved cluster), prod-us-east-1, model routing policy uses auto-fallback: primary -> quantized-gpu-fallback -> cpu-fallback.
- Autoscaler set: min_replicas=2, max_replicas=16, scale_window=30s.

Steps to repr...

title: Tenant burst traffic caused cascading 502s at API proxy and unhealthy upstreams leading to runtime restarts
preview: AtlasRetail reported a sudden increase in 502 errors for their production routes after a traffic surge. Errors are observed at the API proxy layer with upstreams marked unhealthy and several serving runtime worker processes restarting under load. Customer reports higher latency and partial request failures across th...
text: AtlasRetail reported a sudden increase in 502 errors for their production routes after a traffic surge. Errors are observed at the API proxy layer with upstreams marked unhealthy and several serving runtime worker processes restarting under load. Customer reports higher latency and partial request failures across their pinned model routes.

title: Transparent midstream proxy insertion causing serialized streaming and long tail latency between APAC and EU
preview: Issue summary: Since 2026-03-12 03:20 UTC StreamlineAI sees high p95/p99 latency and streaming timeouts for inference requests originating in APAC and routed to eu-west. Symptoms point to an intermediate transparent proxy that is serializing HTTP/2 streams and introducing head-of-line delays; streaming responses are...
text:
Issue summary: Since 2026-03-12 03:20 UTC StreamlineAI sees high p95/p99 latency and streaming timeouts for inference requests originating in APAC and routed to eu-west. Symptoms point to an intermediate transparent proxy that is serializing HTTP/2 streams and introducing head-of-line delays; streaming responses are returned in full chunks rather than interleaved tokens, causing user-observed stalls and occasional 504s at the application layer. Impact: customer-facing production service degradation, active customer requests timing out or experiencing multi-second tail latency, increased error rates in their monitoring. Environment: production, apac-east region for client egress, destination region eu-west hosting model endpoints.

Expected behavior: streaming responses should arrive token-by-token (low TTFB, steady token stream). Observed behavior: initial handshake fine, then long pa...

title: Sustained APAC inference p99 spike: geo-proxy sticky-session fallback causing request queueing
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer Brushwell Analytics reports sustained p99 inference latency increase (from ~350ms to 2.5–4s) and occasional request timeouts for requests routed to apac-east.

Impact:
- Production inference for multiple endpoints degraded for Brushwell (dedicated account).
- Client sees increased API latency...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer Brushwell Analytics reports sustained p99 inference latency increase (from ~350ms to 2.5–4s) and occasional request timeouts for requests routed to apac-east.

Impact:
- Production inference for multiple endpoints degraded for Brushwell (dedicated account).
- Client sees increased API latency and intermittent 504s; affects real-time scoring pipelines.

Environment:
- Redwood hosted, dedicated capacity for Brushwell in apac-east region.
- Observed across multiple model variants pinned to redwood-v2-medium.

Steps to reproduce (from customer):
1) Send streaming chat completion requests to customer key scoped to apac-east.
2) Observe p95/p99 token latencies and occasional streaming disconnects after 2–4s.
3) Repeatedly repros ~20% of requests during window between 2026-03-12T02:10Z and ongoing.

Observed logs / telemetry snippets:

title: Synchronous schema ping from function proxy adds blocking validation hop — p99 end-to-end latency spikes and 10s timeouts for dedicated customer
preview: Issue summary: Customer ScribeAI reports sudden increase in end-to-end request latency (p95/p99) for short chat completions after a recent rollout of a validation change in the function-proxy. Impact: Customer-facing streaming sessions experience long tails and periodic 10s request timeouts. This has escalated to de...
text:
Issue summary: Customer ScribeAI reports sudden increase in end-to-end request latency (p95/p99) for short chat completions after a recent rollout of a validation change in the function-proxy. Impact: Customer-facing streaming sessions experience long tails and periodic 10s request timeouts. This has escalated to degraded UX for interactive flows.

Observed behavior: During short prompts (<= 64 tokens) we observe p99 latency rising from ~220ms to ~2.1s and occasional 10s timeouts. Traces show an extra synchronous RPC from function-proxy to the schema-validator service during the tool/function call orchestration. The RPC performs a schema/contract validation and an indirect K/V metadata lookup which adds blocking I/O on the request path.

Expected behavior: Schema validation should be async or performed off the hot path (prefetch/validate-on-register) so that per-request latency remain...

title: SCIM PATCH Content-Type discrepancy causes role cache divergence and intermittent 403s for recently deprovisioned admins
preview: Customer reports intermittent 403s for users whose admin role was removed in the IdP. The deprovisioning event is visible in the customer's SCIM logs, but Redwood's role cache retains the admin binding for ~30-90s causing a short window of authorization flip (allowed -> denied) depending on which token/session path ...
text: Customer reports intermittent 403s for users whose admin role was removed in the IdP. The deprovisioning event is visible in the customer's SCIM logs, but Redwood's role cache retains the admin binding for ~30-90s causing a short window of authorization flip (allowed -> denied) depending on which token/session path is used. This is causing automated job failures and confusion during immediate post-deprovision operations.

text:
Issue summary:
Customer QueryFlow reported a sustained elevation in 5xx responses (~8-12% error rate) beginning 2026-03-13 03:20 UTC during a large embedding ingestion job. Errors manifested as 502/503 from API gateway with upstream runtime disconnects and occasional runtime GPU preemptions.

- Ingress: api-gw-v3 (http2 multiplexing enabled), runtime: inference-runtime-v1.28
- Affected models: redwood-embed-v2 (batching for embedding), llama-2-7b-chat (mixed chat traffic)

title: Rapid elevation of 5xx rate during large embedding upload + priority-shed misclassification causing upstream OOMs
preview: Issue summary: Customers reported a sharp increase in 5xx responses (primarily 502/503) starting ~2026-03-13 11:45 UTC. Several dedicated customers (notably NimbleStack) saw higher error rates on embedding and streaming routes during a large bulk embedding upload. Errors correlated with upstream runtime restarts and...
text:
Issue summary: Customers reported a sharp increase in 5xx responses (primarily 502/503) starting ~2026-03-13 11:45 UTC. Several dedicated customers (notably NimbleStack) saw higher error rates on embedding and streaming routes during a large bulk embedding upload. Errors correlated with upstream runtime restarts and OOM-like preemption logs.

Impact: Multiple production customers experienced request failures and increased latency for ~45 minutes. Error rate peaked at ~18% on affected routes (baseline <0.5%). Business impact for NimbleStack: failed job completions and API retries causing billing anomalies.

Environment: prod cluster in us-east (dedicated pool A, customer tenant: nimblestack-ded-1). Affects streaming and /v1/embeddings endpoints.

Observed: 5xx increase, apigw return codes 502/503, runtime processes killed and restarted with GPU OOM flag in driver logs, kvcache eviction...

title: Dashboard CSV snapshot export strips row-level filters for long-range snapshots
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer reports that when they request a CSV snapshot export for a dashboard that includes row-level filters (RBAC-driven dataset visibility + cohort filters) over a time range greater than ~90 days, the exported CSV contains either zero rows or a dataset that ignores row-level filters (showing a glo...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports that when they request a CSV snapshot export for a dashboard that includes row-level filters (RBAC-driven dataset visibility + cohort filters) over a time range greater than ~90 days, the exported CSV contains either zero rows or a dataset that ignores row-level filters (showing a global count).

Impact:
- A Tellus Health analytics team cannot reconcile exported CSVs with the dashboard numbers for monthly/quarterly reporting.
- Potential data leakage risk (exports that ignore row-level visibility) or incorrect zeroed exports for long-range snapshots.
- Blocks the customer's compliance reporting workflow; customer flagged as enterprise SLA-impacting.

Environment:
- Production account for Tellus Health (enterprise tenant) in us-east region.
- Dashboard uses nested cohort filters and organization-scoped row-level ACL via console RBAC.
- Export API called ...

title: Embed dimension negotiation: proxy strips metadata header causing rerank CPU fallback and intermittent empty results
preview:

Impact: Client-facing search experiences occasional...
text:
Issue summary: Since 2026-03-11 22:00 UTC, CortexSearch reports intermittent empty candidate sets and p95 rerank latency spikes for their dedicated pool. The symptoms started shortly after they flipped the proxy compression/fast-path toggle during a traffic spike.

Impact: Client-facing search experiences occasional blank results and multi-second tail latency for rerank calls affecting approx 2-3% of queries; revenue impact unknown but noticeable on monitoring dashboards.

Environment: Dedicated cluster in us-west, vpc-connected private control plane, embed endpoint pinned to embed-small-v2, rerank endpoint pinned to rerank-lite-v1.

Steps to reproduce (customer):

2) Immediately call rerank with the returned embedding payload to /v1/rerank

text:
Issue summary:

title: Sustained elevated 5xx floor for customer during mixed embeddings+chat burst: connection oscillation -> runtime GC/eviction thrash
preview: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-13 18:45 UTC BrightDocs reported persistent ~5-8% 5xx rate on mixed-embedding and chat routes for their tenant. Errors manifest as 502/503 from api-gateway and upstream runtime connection errors. Impact: production traffic for BrightDocs degraded, intermittent user-facing failures for...
text:
Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-13 18:45 UTC BrightDocs reported persistent ~5-8% 5xx rate on mixed-embedding and chat routes for their tenant. Errors manifest as 502/503 from api-gateway and upstream runtime connection errors. Impact: production traffic for BrightDocs degraded, intermittent user-facing failures for document ingestion and chat features. Environment: prod-us-east, enterprise tenant (BrightDocs), traffic mix: 60% small chat requests, 40% batched embedding uploads (small/medium).

Observed behavior: When BrightDocs client increases concurrent connections and aggressively reconnects after failed requests, api-gateway 5xx floor rises and remains elevated for ~10-30 minutes before gradually recovering. During elevated period: runtime worker logs show repeated small process OOMs, kvcache eviction storms, and frequent shard reloads (cold-shard events). Apigw logs show 'upstre...

title: Ephemeral port aliasing across multi-NAT egress causes TLS SNI mismatch and intermittent presigned artifact 403s
preview: DatumStream private deployment (VPCE-enabled) started seeing intermittent 403s from presigned S3 URLs used by the serving runtime to stream model shards. text: DatumStream private deployment (VPCE-enabled) started seeing intermittent 403s from presigned S3 URLs used by the serving runtime to stream model shards. The errors are not consistent per-shard; same shard will sometimes succeed and sometimes return 403s for the same task. Client-side retries succeed most of the time. Customer observed increased 5xxs and request retries cascading into throttling-related symptoms.

title: SSE TLS 0-RTT resumption during edge handoff produces partial markdown code fence and drops trailing list items

text:
Issue summary:
Customer experiences truncated SSE streams when TLS 1.3 0-RTT (early data) is used and the client path migrates across edge nodes. The truncation consistently removes the closing markdown code fence (```), and drops the final 1-3 list items from enumerations. Impact:
- Customer's live documentation generator (NoteWave) shows malformed Markdown and missing bullet points in ~4% of streaming sessions during peak traffic.
- Affects user-facing web previews; breaks downstream parsers that expect closed code blocks.
Environment:
- Customer: NoteWave Labs (dedicated tenant)
- API: Hosted Redwood SSE endpoint via api.redwood.ai (prod-us-east)
- Model: gpt-redwood-3b-v1 pinned version 2026-02-25
- Network: client mobile app on LTE -> public mobile NAT -> CDN edge -> Redwood edge proxy -> serving pool
Steps to reproduce (customer-provided):
1) Use mobile app with TLS 1.3 0-RTT en...

title: Streaming completions time out (504) when client-side HTTP/2 WINDOW_UPDATE is not sent by corporate proxy
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer AcmeFin reports repeated streaming completions from our hosted API returning 504s after long tails of silence. This appears only for some users behind a corporate network appliance.

- ~30 requests/hr from AcmeFin experience 40-120s stalls then 504s when generating long completions (>...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer AcmeFin reports repeated streaming completions from our hosted API returning 504s after long tails of silence. This appears only for some users behind a corporate network appliance.

Impact:
- ~30 requests/hr from AcmeFin experience 40-120s stalls then 504s when generating long completions (>512 tokens).
- End-user UX broken for interactive flows.
- Customer is enterprise tier; impact threatens a contract renewal conversation.

Environment:
- Prod edge (us-east) -> API Gateway (HTTP/2 enabled) -> NGINX sidecars -> serving-runtime GPU pool.
- Observed on models: gpt-open-13b, redwood-rl-7b.

Steps to reproduce (approx):
1) Customer client opens HTTP/2 connection to /v1/stream with streaming=true.
2) Server starts emitting many small DATA frames (KV-warmup + prefix output) over the stream for ~30-90s.
3) Corporate proxy between client and our edge silently fails ...

text:
Issue summary: Customer BrightChat reports a sustained elevation in 5xx responses beginning 2026-03-12 02:10 UTC during large embedding multipart uploads. Impact: multiple clients seeing ~15-30% failed requests (5xx) for embedding endpoints; customer observed timeouts and client-side retry storms. Environment: prod us-east; self-serve tenant; burst of concurrent multipart uploads (~100 parallel multipart sessions from a single customer API key). Steps to reproduce:
1) Use BrightChat embedding bulk uploader with 100 parallel workers, each submitting multipart upload with ~12MB parts (total ~120MB per session).
2) Observe API gateway accept-queue growth and some requests truncated by body parser.
3) After sustained churn (~3–5 minutes), upstream serving-runtime processes restart (watchdog), causing 502/503/504 depending on stage.
Observed behavior: apigw emits 502->503 waves; logs show ...

title: Burst of 5xx (mostly 503/502) traced to runtime threadpool starvation from hedged streaming/prefetch traffic
preview: Issue summary: Starting ~2026-03-12 20:15 UTC we observed a sustained uptick in 5xx errors (predominantly 503 and 502) for a Dedicated customer (BrightWrite Inc.) in us-east. Errors correlate with a heavy number of long-lived streaming sessions that use hedged prefetch requests. Impact: API calls to generation/chat ...
text: Issue summary: Starting ~2026-03-12 20:15 UTC we observed a sustained uptick in 5xx errors (predominantly 503 and 502) for a Dedicated customer (BrightWrite Inc.) in us-east. Errors correlate with a heavy number of long-lived streaming sessions that use hedged prefetch requests. Impact: API calls to generation/chat endpoints intermittently return 502/503; streaming sessions abruptly close; customers report degraded latency and dropped responses. Initial hypothesis: runtime threadpool exhaustion / blocking IO in prefetch path leading to upstream failures at the API gateway.

title: Catalog sync drift caused wrong model routing, quality regression, and enterprise customer escalation
preview:
Issue summary:
On 2026-03-10 at ~09:23 UTC we observed a large increase in customer-reported hallucinations and unrelated completions for Fresco Retail. Root cause traced to catalog-sync drift: the model-catalog service had an out-of-sync cache window and continued advertising an older (incompatible) variant for the...
text:
Issue summary:
On 2026-03-10 at ~09:23 UTC we observed a large increase in customer-reported hallucinations and unrelated completions for Fresco Retail. Root cause traced to catalog-sync drift: the model-catalog service had an out-of-sync cache window and continued advertising an older (incompatible) variant for the 'product-description' route, causing requests to be routed to gptx-13b-v2-quant instead of the intended gptx-13b-v2 (non-quantized). This introduced repeated generation artifacts and a measurable quality regression for Fresco's production traffic.

Impact:
- Fresco Retail (enterprise) experienced elevated hallucination rate and semantic errors across catalog-enrichment requests for ~2 hours.
- API response quality SLO degraded (precision drop) and several customers opened escalations with support and account team.
- Billing exposure: some requests were served by a higher-c...

title: Ephemeral driver IO attach lag causing reserved-pool throughput sag
preview:
Issue summary:
An intermittent surge of host-level IO activity (short-lived bursts from a background checkpoint writer) is causing large GPU attach/handshake latencies in a reserved pool. The attach delays lead to the autoscaler and scheduler seeing transient capacity unavailability, which triggers rapid slot reassi...
text:
Issue summary:
An intermittent surge of host-level IO activity (short-lived bursts from a background checkpoint writer) is causing large GPU attach/handshake latencies in a reserved pool. The attach delays lead to the autoscaler and scheduler seeing transient capacity unavailability, which triggers rapid slot reassignments and an observable throughput sag for the customer's reserved capacity window.

Impact:
- Streamline AI reports elevated p95/p99 latency for batched requests during a 30-90s window several times per hour.
- Throughput drop: observed 20-40% drop in steady-state token throughput for the affected partition during bursts.
- Application-level errors: a small percentage (~0.2%) of requests return 5xx attach-timeouts before autoscaler fallback kicks in.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: reserved-cluster-14 (prod-us-west)
- Host type: 8x A100 (mainly homogeneous) with local NV...

title: Quota boundary and throttle-shepherding guidance for mixed bulk + realtime workload

text:
Issue summary: Customer BeaconMaps seeing recurring 429s during daily bulk index refresh and low-latency routing for map tile annotations. Impact: Intermittent 429s causing failed embedders and delayed annotations in production (~18% of index refresh jobs fail).

Environment: Dedicated pool (dedicated-beacon-3), prod us-west. Heavy mixed workload: scheduled bulk embeddings (multipart uploads) + real-time annotation calls from mobile clients.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Trigger nightly bulk ingest for 80k documents via multipart upload with 16 parallel workers.
2) While ingest runs, send ~200 low-latency chat/annotation requests per second pinned to the same model variant.
3) Observe rate-limit headers and return codes.

Observed behavior: Throttles (429) occur predominantly on multipart upload endpoints and the streaming annotation calls report Retry-After of 5-7s. Customer reports that r...

title: Dedicated capacity pulse: sudden p50/p99 latency increases correlated with misaligned sidecar heartbeats
preview: Issue summary: Customer Lumina Analytics reported a recurring, short-lived pulse in p50 and p99 latency across their dedicated pool starting 2026-03-09 14:00 UTC. Impact: user-facing API calls (generation and streaming) saw p50 increase from ~120ms to ~320ms and p99 spikes to 1.2s for 10-30s windows, causing degrade...
text: Issue summary: Customer Lumina Analytics reported a recurring, short-lived pulse in p50 and p99 latency across their dedicated pool starting 2026-03-09 14:00 UTC. Impact: user-facing API calls (generation and streaming) saw p50 increase from ~120ms to ~320ms and p99 spikes to 1.2s for 10-30s windows, causing degraded interactive experience. Pattern: pulses occur roughly every 5-12 minutes and last ~10-30s; throughput (tokens/s) drops by 20-40% during pulses. Initial hypothesis: interference from background sidecar telemetry/metrics emitters causing transient scheduler/attach delays and autoscaler sensing noise.

Customer reports an unexpected prorated invoice line that appears to bill for ~18,000 toolcall invocations during a two-hour window on 2026-03-06. - Customer is an enterprise Private deployment customer with reserved hours; unexpected charge impacts finance reconciliation and internal chargeback.

- Region: us-west-2.

text:
Issue summary:
ScribeAI reported a persistent elevated 502/503 error rate starting 2026-03-12 02:10 UTC across multiple endpoints. Errors present as short-lived waves that plateau rather than sharp spikes — API gateway returning 502/503 while upstream runtimes report long TLS handshake queues and delayed worker start.

- Streaming sessions intermittently failed to establish with 502s.
- Latency for successful requests increased by ~2x during plateau periods.

- Models: gptx-13b-v2 pinned variant
- Redwood stack: api-gateway -> ephemeral-proxy group -> serving-runtime (dedicated GPU pool)

text: Issue summary: Customer reports SSE chat sessions receiving an initial stream of tokens and then entering a prolonged silent period (~30-90s) without socket close or error. Client eventually times out. Impact: High — production traffic for Nimbus Finance experienced multiple user-facing interruptions for multi-turn assistant sessions during peak business hours. Environment: Dedicated pool (reserved capacity) for Nimbus Finance in us-west-2 running quantized llama-2-13b model variant. Observed at ~2026-03-12 15:04 PDT during a staged live model swap triggered by platform rollout automation.

title: Stealth health-sweep triggered autoscaler hysteresis and ephemeral GPU deficit in reserved pool
preview:
Issue summary:
A GlyphHealth dedicated pool experienced a rapid drop in available GPU attachments for ~12 minutes during a midnight maintenance window. The incident coincided with an internal health-sweep job that opened short-lived monitoring connections across many nodes. Autoscaler entered a hysteresis loop (rapi...
text:
Issue summary:
A GlyphHealth dedicated pool experienced a rapid drop in available GPU attachments for ~12 minutes during a midnight maintenance window. The incident coincided with an internal health-sweep job that opened short-lived monitoring connections across many nodes. Autoscaler entered a hysteresis loop (rapid shrink/grow backoff) and failed to reacquire slots quickly, causing a P50/P99 throughput dip for the customer's reserved routes.

Impact:
- Customer observed increased p50 latency from 120ms -> 420ms and p99 spike to 3.2s on reserved-batched routes for ~12 minutes.
- ~18% of requested attachments failed and were retried, causing additional queueing and cost overhead.
- No customer data leak; requests succeeded after retries. Customer reported degraded UX for core inference flows and opened support during incident.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool (GlyphHealth - dedicated co...

title: SomaHealth: semantic accuracy drop after enabling adaptive async batching + 4-bit quant + KV TTL eviction
preview: Issue opened by SomaHealth CS: after enabling the new adaptive async batching policy (per-request grouping) together with the new 4-bit quantization profile and the kv-cache TTL eviction (LRU-timeout=30s) for tenant somahealth-prod, several endpoints show increased semantic errors and hallucinations for a critical c...
text: Issue opened by SomaHealth CS: after enabling the new adaptive async batching policy (per-request grouping) together with the new 4-bit quantization profile and the kv-cache TTL eviction (LRU-timeout=30s) for tenant somahealth-prod, several endpoints show increased semantic errors and hallucinations for a critical clinical extraction prompt set. Customer observed sudden mismatch between human-labeled expected extraction values and model outputs starting 2026-03-11 22:00 UTC after a config rollout. They want guidance for immediate safe rollback and root-cause investigation.

title: Tenant-induced pipeline IO spill causing autoscaler latency and p99 throughput degradation in reserved pool
preview: Issue summary: Multiple LexiHealth tenants on reserved capacity experienced sudden p99 throughput regressions and elevated tail latency beginning 2026-03-10 14:12 UTC. Symptoms include stalled batch queues, autoscaler taking 30-90s to react, and error bursts of 502/504 on long streaming requests. Impact: LexiHealth ...
text: Issue summary: Multiple LexiHealth tenants on reserved capacity experienced sudden p99 throughput regressions and elevated tail latency beginning 2026-03-10 14:12 UTC. Symptoms include stalled batch queues, autoscaler taking 30-90s to react, and error bursts of 502/504 on long streaming requests. Impact: LexiHealth reported degraded user-facing response times for inference flows tied to clinical note summarization, causing timeouts and retries in their app. Timeline: 14:12 UTC spike in IO wait and NVMe device queue depth observed; autoscaler attempted scale-up but new allocations delayed due to slot fragmentation; 14:27 UTC temporary mitigation applied (tenant soft-rate-limit). Hypothesis: A noisy tenant generated high small-request fanout (many short streams) that induced storage-io pressure on the node local NVMe, which cascaded into serving runtime stalls and autoscaler misjudging ...

text:
Issue summary:
Customer (FinSecure Analytics, enterprise) requests cryptographically verifiable evidence that audit events delivered to their S3-compatible archive are tenant-scoped, immutable, and traceable back to the originating request IDs in Redwood’s audit pipeline.

Impact:
- Customer requires this evidence as part of an external SOC 2 + sector-specific audit; failure to provide suitable artifacts will delay attestation.
- They specifically need proofs that (1) raw events are stored with a content hash manifest, (2) each archived object contains metadata linking to the original Redwood request_id and tenant_id, and (3) the archive retention and object-immutability controls are enforced for the requested date range (90 days).

Environment:
- Redwood prod (us-east-1) -> customer S3-compatible endpoint (OCI-compatible object store supplied by customer).

title: Elevated P95 latency and 504s on dedicated pool — node-level interference + autoscale gap
preview: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-10 08:22 UTC, Ledgerly reported intermittent p95 latency spikes and an increase in 504/timeout responses for requests routed to their dedicated GPU pool in eu-west-1. Surface metrics show sustained p50 ~150ms but p95 jumping from ~220ms baseline to 1.2-1.6s during spikes, and a burst ...
text: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-10 08:22 UTC, Ledgerly reported intermittent p95 latency spikes and an increase in 504/timeout responses for requests routed to their dedicated GPU pool in eu-west-1. Surface metrics show sustained p50 ~150ms but p95 jumping from ~220ms baseline to 1.2-1.6s during spikes, and a burst of 504s coincident with those spikes. Impact: customer-facing feature degraded; higher error rate and user-visible latency. Hypothesis: localized GPU contention (noisy neighbor) on one or two nodes combined with autoscaler not reacting quickly enough (min_replicas/config mismatch), leading to request queueing and timeouts.

title: Guidance requested: shard-aware idempotency strategy for multiplexed tool chains across Go/TS SDKs
preview:
Issue summary:
NimbusPayments is implementing a high-throughput payment-validation workflow that orchestrates three external tools in sequence (risk-check, account-lookup, settlement-prep). They use multiplexed client connections and the TS client on the web front-end plus a Go worker pool for server-side orchestrat...
text:
Issue summary:
NimbusPayments is implementing a high-throughput payment-validation workflow that orchestrates three external tools in sequence (risk-check, account-lookup, settlement-prep). They use multiplexed client connections and the TS client on the web front-end plus a Go worker pool for server-side orchestration. They are asking for best-practice guidance for designing an idempotency strategy that avoids collisions and duplicate tool-side effects when requests are sharded across multiple worker processes and both SDKs are used.

Impact:
- Potential duplicate downstream charges or duplicate webhook posts observed in a narrow subset of high-concurrency runs (approx 0.01% of transactions).
- Customer is hesitant to roll to 100% for a dedicated flow because of regulatory payout concerns.

What they want:
- Concrete, shard-aware idempotency key design patterns that work when request...

title: Resume duplication and partial-final-choice when SSE streams are proxied over HTTP/2 multiplexed connections

The client receives a duplicate mid-sentence fragment followed immediately by an end event that omits the last ...
text:
Issue summary: Customer reports intermittent duplication of the last streamed segment and a truncated final choice after client-triggered reconnects when SSE is proxied through an HTTP/2 multiplexing path. The client receives a duplicate mid-sentence fragment followed immediately by an end event that omits the last few words.

Impact: Affects real-time UX for Acme's live summarization feature. Customers see duplicated words and cut-off sentences ~1-3% of long (>10k token) streams. Reported in production us-east region and causing apparent hallucination-like artifacts in final outputs (customer counts these as correctness failures).

Environment: prod / us-east behind CDN/edge + ALB -> Envoy ingress -> serving runtime. Observed with SSE (text/event-stream) over HTTP/2 multiplexed connections from edge to internal proxies. Affected models: redwood-14b-v1 (rt-compat) and redwood-7b-v1 in...

title: Long-lived client connections causing API gateway accept-queue starvation and upstream runtime crashloop with elevated 5xx
preview:

Customer QueryWell reported a rapid increase in 5xx responses starting 2026-03-12 10:20 UTC for production traffic routed to their dedicated pool. Symptoms include API gateway 502/503 spikes coincident with long-lived streaming connections from a handful of client IPs. Upstream runtimes entered crashl...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer QueryWell reported a rapid increase in 5xx responses starting 2026-03-12 10:20 UTC for production traffic routed to their dedicated pool. Symptoms include API gateway 502/503 spikes coincident with long-lived streaming connections from a handful of client IPs. Upstream runtimes entered crashloop/eviction cycles and GPU memory fragmentation grew steadily during the window.

Impact:
- QueryWell observed ~18% of requests returning 5xx over a 40-minute period, primarily streaming endpoints.
- Latency increased for non-streaming requests by ~30%.
- Incident impacted a single dedicated pool (customer-dedicated) — no cross-customer outage reported.

Environment:
- prod/us-east (customer dedicated capacity)
- Affected runtimes: r10-r16 in dedicated pool qwe-us-east-02
- Models in use: redwood-alpha-13b-q4 (inference with KV cache and streaming enabled)

Steps to reprod...

title: Microburst metering lag causing invoice smoothing discrepancy
preview: Issue summary: Customer reports a $3,820 negative adjustment on invoice #INV-2026-03-08 after a scheduled smoothing job; LexiTech observed credits applied then revoked the next hour. Impact: Billing inaccuracy and customer concern; affecting monthly close for LexiTech (enterprise) and blocking finance reconciliation...
text: Issue summary: Customer reports a $3,820 negative adjustment on invoice #INV-2026-03-08 after a scheduled smoothing job; LexiTech observed credits applied then revoked the next hour. Impact: Billing inaccuracy and customer concern; affecting monthly close for LexiTech (enterprise) and blocking finance reconciliation. Environment: prod (us-west primary, with replica in eu-west). Timeline: bursts of high embedding traffic (microbursts) on 2026-03-07 23:40-23:47 UTC; billing smoothing job ran at 2026-03-08 00:00 UTC. Observed symptom: provisional credits created then dropped when smoothing completed, resulting in final invoice delta. Customer-facing impact: incorrect invoice line and confusion; customer requests reversal and justification for adjustments.
